channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Earns $190M in 5 Days at U.S. Box Office
item.content:encoded:
The Super Mario Galaxy Movie has grossed nearly $200 million in its first 5 days at the U.S. box office. Now, there’s new data that shows how helpful the latest Nintendo and Illumination crossover has been for movie theaters.

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie opened in theaters on April 1. A new report from Deadline notes that domestic 5-day earnings totaled $190 million. Globally, the film has earned $372.5 million. Statistically, it’s the second biggest 3-day opening for an Illumination title behind the original Super Mario Bros. Movie in 2023.

Further Proof That The Super Mario Galaxy Movie Is a Massive Success at the Box Office

The success of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie has helped the domestic box office surpass the $2 billion mark in the first quarter of the year. Additionally, the movie reportedly attracted 62% of non-frequent moviegoers.

AMC CEO Adam Aron gushed about the movie’s success. He was quoted as saying that the movie delivered on all accounts, with families and younger watchers showing up in huge numbers.

“It’s the highest-grossing opening weekend of 2026 so far. As we look ahead to the rest
of the spring and summer, we see one blockbuster after another soon to be released, which leads to the obvious conclusion that the trajectory for our industry is very encouraging.”

The movie wasn’t necessarily a smash hit with critics, but Mario continues to draw crowds even after celebrating 40 years as a standalone video game star. Of course, the most recent movie also featured the likes of Yoshi, Luigi, and Star Fox’s Fox McCloud.

Pikmin were also featured in a cameo, but if Shigeru Miyamoto had his way, they would be featured in everything Nintendo does. Learn more about that here.

Have you seen The Super Mario Galaxy Movie yet? If so, what did you think of the film? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starfield’s Terran Armada Achievements Uncover Secret Features
item.content:encoded:
Terran Armada drops in just two days, bringing Starfield fans everywhere the long-awaited second major expansion for Bethesda Game Studios’ space-faring epic. It has been more than a year since the last expansion, Shattered Space, broadened the game’s horizons, but until just a couple of weeks ago, Bethesda was standing silent on the next expansion.

We know now it’s called Terran Armada, and we also know what the point of the expansion is. And, thanks to recent achievement lists being posted online, we know the DLC’s hidden features that are yet to be revealed.

Don’t read on any further if you don’t want the core elements of Terran Armada uncovered.

Starfield Achievements Reveal Unseen Elements

There are a whopping twenty achievements (and now, of course, PlayStation Trophies) inbound to Starfield for Terran Armada and the Free Lanes update, which drops in just a couple of days on April 7.

These achievements, which are easily viewable on Steam at your own risk, detail some important features of Starfield that we’ve not yet been privy to. Bethesda Game Studios might have revealed stacks of information about the next update, but not everything was explained.

For instance, there’s ‘This for That’, an achievement that requires players to build and hand over an outpost to a faction, which is an all-new feature. There’s also a triple pack achievement of sorts related to the new ‘Incursion’ mechanic in Terran Armada. This will see players engage in massive space battles, and there’s an achievement for completing five, then fifteen, then thirty Incursions.

Delta, the new robotic companion introduced with Terran Armada, has a couple of achievements. There’s one for fully upgrading them, and another to ‘convince Delta that you are worthy of the title Captain’.

What has fans most excited is the arrival of a new city (or a super-sized ship of sorts) in Terran Armada. There’s an achievement called ‘Master of Magnetism’ that requires players to use a magnet to destroy a robot in New Babylon.

Here’s the full list of achievements coming with Terran Armada and Free Lanes on April 7:

  • Lost Luxury – Complete Lost Luxury
  • Into the VOID – Complete Into the VOID
  • Battle of the Unifier – Complete Battle of the Unifier
  • This for That – Build out and hand over an outpost to a faction
  • Greater Than – Convince Delta that you are worthy of the title Captain
  • Less Than – Prove the superiority of artificial life
  • Incursion Fighter – Clear out 5 Incursions
  • Incursion Exterminator – Clear out 15 Incursions
  • Incursion Eliminator – Clear out 30 Incursions
  • Overclocked – Fully upgrade Delta
  • Half the Battle – Let Sarah Morgan know about her action figure
  • Fully Equipped – Find a spaceship equipment module schematic and install an equipment module on your ship
  • Top Tier – Apply a Rank 4 Legendary Effect to a piece of your equipment
  • Just Like Grandma Used to Make – Eat the questionable food offered by a spacefaring old woman
  • They’re Not Toys! – Collect all action figures
  • Contract Killer – Kill 10 targets for the Arbitrator
  • Stay of Execution – Stop Bernhard from killing Caruso
  • Master of Magnetism – Use a magnet to destroy the robot for Ivica in New Babylon
  • Riddle Management – Prevent any deaths by answering riddles
  • Quantum Hoarder – Retrieve your inventory from another universe

Are you eager to unlock the full list of achievements in Starfield? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that ARC Raiders has more fixes inbound

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
